rawText:
People have always tried to attract romantic partners by displaying wealth, beauty, or social standing. In ancient Rome, poets wrote detailed guides on personal grooming and charm. They advised readers to keep their teeth clean, remove body hair, and develop a sun-kissed appearance. Much of this advice sounds remarkably familiar today. Modern dating blogs and social media influencers offer strikingly similar tips. The desire to look attractive and impress a potential partner has not changed much across two thousand years of human history.

What has changed, however, is the relationship between money and marriage. During America's Gilded Age in the late 1800s, industrial tycoons built enormous fortunes while ordinary workers struggled in poverty. Wealthy men of that era often married much younger women, and high society treated marriage as a transaction involving status and financial security. Literature from the period captures this dynamic vividly. Novels set among the New York elite describe a world where dinner parties and social customs served as tools for controlling who married whom. The wealthy kept tight boundaries around their circle, and romantic choice was heavily shaped by class and money.

For most of the twentieth century, this pattern continued in a milder form. Data on American marriages show that in 1980, very rich men were significantly more likely than average to have wives ten or more years younger. The stereotype of the older, powerful man with a glamorous young spouse was grounded in real numbers. But recent decades have brought a dramatic shift. By 2024, the wealthiest men no longer stood out from the general population in terms of spousal age gaps. The old model had quietly faded.

Several forces drove this change. As more women pursued higher education and built independent careers, they gained the freedom to choose partners based on shared interests and compatible lifestyles rather than financial need. People who spend years in university tend to form relationships with classmates close to their own age. Education and evolving social attitudes have replaced wealth as the primary factors shaping who pairs up with whom. Meanwhile, women who earn high salaries generally do not seek significantly younger husbands, suggesting that economic power alone does not automatically produce large age gaps in either direction.

From Roman grooming manuals to Gilded Age marriage markets to modern data on couples, one thread runs through every era: people care deeply about attracting and keeping a partner. The specific rules change with each generation, but the underlying human impulse remains constant. What is different today is that partnership increasingly rests on equality and personal connection rather than on displays of wealth and status. That quiet revolution may be the most significant shift in the long history of romance.

overview.title: Love, Money, and Status Through the Ages

sentences.original: People have always tried to attract romantic partners by displaying wealth, beauty, or social standing.
sentences.translation: 사람들은 항상 부, 아름다움, 또는 사회적 지위를 과시함으로써 연인을 끌어들이려고 해왔다.

grammarElements.text: by displaying wealth, beauty, or social standing
grammarElements.label: 수단 전치사구 (by + 동명사)

vocabulary.example: Many people meet romantic partners through mutual friends.
The peacock attracts a mate by displaying its colorful feathers.
In many cultures, social standing influences marriage prospects.

expressions.usage: 수단이나 방법을 나타내는 by + 동명사 구문. 영어에서 매우 빈번하게 쓰이는 핵심 패턴

patterns.pattern: [People] have always [past participle] to [verb] by [method]
patterns.explanation: 인류가 역사적으로 줄곧 해온 보편적 행동을 현재완료로 제시하는 도입 구조
examples: Humans have always tried to predict the future by studying patterns.
Communities have always sought to protect themselves by forming alliances.

drillQuestions.question: People have always tried to attract romantic partners by ______ wealth, beauty, or social standing.

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 대주제를 설정하는 도입문. 현재완료(have always tried)가 '인류 보편의 행동'이라는 시간적 범위를 나타낸다. by + 동명사 구문은 영어 학습의 핵심 패턴.

koreanAlignment.en: by displaying wealth, beauty, or social standing
koreanAlignment.ko: 부, 아름다움, 또는 사회적 지위를 과시함으로써

sentences.original: In ancient Rome, poets wrote detailed guides on personal grooming and charm.
sentences.translation: 고대 로마에서 시인들은 개인 치장과 매력에 관한 상세한 안내서를 썼다.

grammarElements.text: detailed guides on personal grooming and charm
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (전치사구 수식)

vocabulary.example: Personal grooming habits vary widely across cultures.
Her natural charm made everyone feel at ease.

expressions.usage: 특정 주제에 대한 지침이나 매뉴얼을 가리킬 때 사용. guide to와도 호환 가능

patterns.pattern: In [historical setting], [people] [past tense verb] [detailed product]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 배경을 제시하고 과거 사람들의 구체적 행동을 서술하는 서사적 패턴
examples: In medieval Europe, monks copied detailed manuscripts on herbal medicine.
In ancient Egypt, priests developed detailed calendars based on star movements.

drillQuestions.question: Poets wrote detailed guides on personal ______ and charm.

sentences.notes: 고대 로마의 구체적 사례로 도입문의 보편적 주장을 뒷받침한다. grooming은 현대에도 자주 사용되는 단어로, 과거와 현재의 연결을 암시.

sentences.original: They advised readers to keep their teeth clean, remove body hair, and develop a sun-kissed appearance.
sentences.translation: 그들은 독자들에게 치아를 깨끗이 유지하고, 체모를 제거하고, 햇볕에 그을린 외모를 가꾸라고 조언했다.

grammarElements.text: to keep their teeth clean, remove body hair, and develop a sun-kissed appearance
grammarElements.label: to부정사 보어 (삼중 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The doctor advised her to rest for a full week.
She returned from vacation with a sun-kissed glow.

expressions.usage: 목적어 뒤에 to부정사가 오는 5형식 구문. recommend, encourage와 유사한 구조

patterns.pattern: They advised [audience] to [action 1], [action 2], and [action 3]
patterns.explanation: 조언의 내용을 삼중 병렬 to부정사로 나열하는 구조. 두 번째, 세 번째 항목에서 to가 생략됨
examples: The trainer advised participants to stretch before exercise, hydrate frequently, and cool down afterward.
The manual advised users to read instructions, check settings, and restart the device.

drillQuestions.question: They advised readers to keep their teeth clean, remove body hair, and develop a ______ appearance.

sentences.notes: 삼중 병렬 구조에서 to가 첫 항목에만 쓰이고 나머지에서는 생략된 점에 주목. sun-kissed는 합성 형용사로, 하이픈 사용이 필수.

sentences.original: Much of this advice sounds remarkably familiar today.
sentences.translation: 이 조언의 상당 부분이 오늘날 놀라울 정도로 익숙하게 들린다.

vocabulary.example: The two cities are remarkably similar in layout and culture.

expressions.usage: 이전에 들어본 것 같거나 친숙한 느낌이 들 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Much of this [noun] sounds remarkably [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 것이 현재 기준으로도 놀라울 정도로 어떤 특성을 지님을 표현하는 연결동사 구조
examples: Much of this research sounds remarkably current.
Much of this philosophy sounds remarkably practical.

drillQuestions.question: Much of this advice sounds ______ familiar today.

sentences.notes: 짧은 전환 문장으로, 고대 로마의 조언이 현대에도 유효함을 독자에게 환기시킨다. sound는 2형식 연결동사(~하게 들리다)로 보어를 취한다.

sentences.original: Modern dating blogs and social media influencers offer strikingly similar tips.
sentences.translation: 현대의 연애 블로그와 소셜 미디어 인플루언서들이 놀라울 만큼 유사한 조언을 제공한다.

grammarElements.text: Modern dating blogs and social media influencers
grammarElements.label: 복합 주어

vocabulary.example: The new building is strikingly different from its surroundings.
Social media influencers shape consumer behavior worldwide.

expressions.usage: 두 대상의 유사성이 예상 밖으로 높을 때 강조하는 표현. remarkably similar와 호환 가능

patterns.pattern: [Modern equivalent] offer strikingly similar [noun]
patterns.explanation: 현대의 대응물이 과거와 놀라울 정도로 유사함을 직접 대조하는 구조
examples: Modern self-help books offer strikingly similar advice to ancient Stoic texts.
Modern fitness programs offer strikingly similar routines to those of Greek athletes.

drillQuestions.question: Modern dating blogs and social media influencers offer ______ similar tips.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 remarkably familiar를 strikingly similar로 다시 강화하여 과거-현재 병행을 부각한다. 두 부사 모두 '놀라울 정도로'의 뉘앙스.

sentences.original: The desire to look attractive and impress a potential partner has not changed much across two thousand years of human history.
sentences.translation: 매력적으로 보이고 잠재적 파트너에게 좋은 인상을 주고 싶은 욕구는 2천 년의 인류 역사에 걸쳐 크게 변하지 않았다.

grammarElements.text: The desire to look attractive and impress a potential partner
grammarElements.label: 주어 (to부정사 수식)

grammarElements.text: across two thousand years of human history
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

vocabulary.example: The company is always looking for potential investors.
He tried hard to impress his interviewers.

expressions.usage: 매우 긴 시간적 범위를 across로 표현하여 보편성을 강조. over, throughout와 유사

patterns.pattern: The desire to [verb] has not changed much across [time span]
patterns.explanation: 특정 욕구나 행동이 긴 시간에 걸쳐 변하지 않았음을 강조하는 첫 문단 마무리 구조
examples: The desire to understand the natural world has not changed much across centuries of scientific progress.
The desire to belong to a community has not changed much across generations.

drillQuestions.question: The desire to look attractive has not changed much ______ two thousand years of human history.

sentences.notes: 첫 문단의 마무리 문장으로, 도입문의 주장을 다시 한번 확인한다. across는 시간적 범위를 공간적 이미지로 표현하는 전치사. to look와 (to) impress가 병렬된 to부정사 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: across two thousand years of human history

sentences.original: What has changed, however, is the relationship between money and marriage.
sentences.translation: 그러나 변한 것은 돈과 결혼 사이의 관계이다.

grammarElements.text: the relationship between money and marriage
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: The relationship between education and income has been widely studied.

expressions.usage: 앞선 내용과 대조하며 새로운 논점으로 전환할 때 사용하는 강조 구문 (의사분열문)

patterns.pattern: What has [past participle], however, is [new topic]
patterns.explanation: 앞 문단에서 '변하지 않은 것'을 논한 뒤 '변한 것'으로 전환하는 의사분열문(pseudo-cleft) 구조
examples: What has improved, however, is the quality of public discourse.
What has shifted, however, is the balance of power between employer and employee.

drillQuestions.question: What has changed, however, ______ the relationship between money and marriage.

sentences.notes: 의사분열문(pseudo-cleft)은 '변한 것은 바로 ~이다'라는 강조 구조. 첫 문단의 '변하지 않은 것'에서 '변한 것'으로 전환하는 핵심 피벗 문장. however가 삽입되어 대조를 명시.

koreanAlignment.en: the relationship between money and marriage

sentences.original: During America's Gilded Age in the late 1800s, industrial tycoons built enormous fortunes while ordinary workers struggled in poverty.
sentences.translation: 1800년대 후반 미국의 도금 시대에, 산업 거물들이 막대한 부를 쌓는 동안 보통 노동자들은 가난 속에서 허덕였다.

grammarElements.text: During America's Gilded Age in the late 1800s
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

grammarElements.text: while ordinary workers struggled in poverty
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

vocabulary.example: The Gilded Age saw rapid economic growth alongside extreme inequality.
Tech tycoons have reshaped the global economy.
Many families lost their fortunes during the financial crisis.

expressions.usage: 경제적 어려움을 겪는 상황을 묘사할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: During [historical period], [group A] [verb-ed] [success] while [group B] [verb-ed] in [hardship]
patterns.explanation: 특정 시대에 두 집단의 극명한 대조를 while로 병렬하는 역사 서술 패턴
examples: During the Industrial Revolution, factory owners accumulated wealth while child laborers suffered in dangerous conditions.
During the dot-com boom, startup founders became millionaires while traditional retailers struggled to survive.

drillQuestions.question: Industrial ______ built enormous fortunes while ordinary workers struggled in poverty.

sentences.notes: Gilded Age는 마크 트웨인이 만든 용어로, 겉은 화려하지만 속은 부패한 시대를 뜻한다. while이 '동시에'와 '대조' 두 기능을 동시에 수행.

koreanAlignment.en: while ordinary workers struggled in poverty
koreanAlignment.ko: 보통 노동자들은 가난 속에서 허덕이는 동안

pronunciationNotes: tycoons [taɪˈkuːnz] -- '타이쿠운즈'로 두 번째 음절 강세와 장모음

sentences.original: Wealthy men of that era often married much younger women, and high society treated marriage as a transaction involving status and financial security.
sentences.translation: 그 시대의 부유한 남성들은 훨씬 젊은 여성과 결혼하는 경우가 많았고, 상류 사회는 결혼을 지위와 경제적 안정이 관련된 거래로 취급했다.

grammarElements.text: marriage as a transaction involving status and financial security
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + as 보어구

vocabulary.example: High society gatherings were exclusive and carefully curated.
Every business transaction was recorded in the ledger.

expressions.usage: 어떤 대상을 특정 범주로 인식하거나 대우할 때 사용하는 5형식 구문

patterns.pattern: [Group] treated [concept] as a [category] involving [elements]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 집단이 특정 개념을 다른 범주로 간주했음을 표현하는 구조. treat A as B는 핵심 5형식 패턴
examples: Victorian society treated education as a privilege involving wealth and connections.
Corporate culture treated loyalty as a currency involving years of service and obedience.

drillQuestions.question: High society treated marriage as a ______ involving status and financial security.

sentences.notes: treat A as B 구문은 '~를 ~로 취급하다'는 뜻으로 영어에서 매우 중요한 패턴. involving은 현재분사로 transaction을 후치 수식.

sentences.original: Literature from the period captures this dynamic vividly.
sentences.translation: 그 시대의 문학 작품은 이러한 역학 관계를 생생하게 포착한다.

vocabulary.example: The photograph captures the emotion of the moment perfectly.
The power dynamic between employer and employee has shifted.
She vividly described the scene from her childhood.

expressions.usage: 문학, 예술 등이 사회적 현상을 잘 반영할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Source] from the period captures [phenomenon] vividly
patterns.explanation: 특정 시대의 자료가 당시 현상을 잘 보여준다는 것을 간결하게 소개하는 전환 패턴
examples: Art from the period captures the social unrest vividly.
Journalism from the period captures the public mood vividly.

drillQuestions.question: Literature from the period captures this dynamic ______.

sentences.notes: 짧은 전환 문장으로, 역사적 설명에서 문학적 근거로 넘어가는 다리 역할. 현재시제(captures)는 문학 작품이 여전히 유효한 기록임을 암시하는 역사적 현재.

sentences.original: Novels set among the New York elite describe a world where dinner parties and social customs served as tools for controlling who married whom.
sentences.translation: 뉴욕 상류층을 배경으로 한 소설들은 만찬회와 사교 관습이 누가 누구와 결혼하는지를 통제하는 도구로 기능했던 세계를 묘사한다.

grammarElements.text: Novels set among the New York elite
grammarElements.label: 주어 (과거분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: a world where dinner parties and social customs served as tools for controlling who married whom
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

vocabulary.example: The country's political elite controlled most of the nation's wealth.
Social customs vary greatly from one country to another.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것이 특정 목적을 달성하기 위한 수단이 되었음을 표현
who-whom 대비로 주어/목적어를 구분하는 격식적 표현. 구어에서는 who...who로도 사용

patterns.pattern: [Works] set among [group] describe a world where [practices] served as tools for [controlling/achieving] [outcome]
patterns.explanation: 특정 집단을 배경으로 한 작품이 묘사하는 세계를 소개하는 문학 분석 패턴
examples: Films set among the British aristocracy describe a world where etiquette served as a tool for maintaining class boundaries.
Memoirs set among immigrant communities describe a world where language served as a tool for building identity.

drillQuestions.question: Dinner parties and social customs served as ______ for controlling who married whom.

sentences.notes: set among은 과거분사의 후치 수식으로 '~을 배경으로 한'이라는 뜻. who married whom에서 whom은 목적격 관계대명사로, 격식적 문체의 표지.

koreanAlignment.en: Novels set among the New York elite

pronunciationNotes: elite [ɪˈliːt] -- '일리이트'로 두 번째 음절 강세와 장모음

sentences.original: The wealthy kept tight boundaries around their circle, and romantic choice was heavily shaped by class and money.
sentences.translation: 부유층은 자신들의 사교계 주변에 엄격한 경계를 유지했고, 연애의 선택은 계급과 돈에 의해 크게 좌우되었다.

grammarElements.text: romantic choice was heavily shaped by class and money
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (수동태)

vocabulary.example: Setting clear boundaries is essential in any healthy relationship.
Our personalities are shaped by both genetics and environment.

expressions.usage: the + 형용사로 특정 집단 전체를 지칭하는 표현. the rich, the poor, the elderly 등과 같은 패턴

patterns.pattern: The [adjective as noun group] kept tight [noun] around [possession], and [concept] was heavily shaped by [factors]
patterns.explanation: 특정 집단의 배타적 행동과 그로 인한 구조적 결과를 등위절로 연결하는 패턴
examples: The powerful kept tight control around their territory, and political discourse was heavily shaped by fear and loyalty.
The privileged kept tight networks around their institutions, and opportunity was heavily shaped by birth and background.

drillQuestions.question: Romantic choice was heavily ______ by class and money.

sentences.notes: the wealthy는 'the + 형용사 = 복수 집단' 패턴의 전형적 예. 두 번째 절의 수동태(was shaped by)는 행위의 주체보다 영향을 받는 대상에 초점을 둔다.

koreanAlignment.en: kept tight boundaries around their circle
was heavily shaped by class and money

sentences.original: For most of the twentieth century, this pattern continued in a milder form.
sentences.translation: 20세기 대부분 동안 이 패턴은 더 완화된 형태로 지속되었다.

grammarElements.text: For most of the twentieth century
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

vocabulary.example: A similar pattern has been observed in other countries.
The symptoms appeared in a milder form than expected.

expressions.usage: 이전과 같은 현상이지만 강도가 약해졌음을 표현

patterns.pattern: For most of [time period], this [noun] continued in a [adjective-er] form
patterns.explanation: 특정 기간 동안 기존 현상이 변형된 형태로 지속되었음을 나타내는 시간적 연속 패턴
examples: For most of the postwar era, this inequality continued in a subtler form.
For most of the decade, this trend continued in a less visible form.

drillQuestions.question: For most of the twentieth century, this pattern continued in a ______ form.

sentences.notes: 새 문단의 도입문으로, 앞 문단의 도금 시대 패턴이 20세기에도 이어졌음을 간결하게 연결한다. in a milder form은 '완전히 사라진 것은 아니지만 약해졌다'는 뉘앙스.

koreanAlignment.en: For most of the twentieth century

sentences.original: Data on American marriages show that in 1980, very rich men were significantly more likely than average to have wives ten or more years younger.
sentences.translation: 미국 결혼에 관한 데이터는 1980년에 매우 부유한 남성이 평균보다 10년 이상 어린 아내를 둘 가능성이 현저히 높았음을 보여준다.

grammarElements.text: that in 1980, very rich men were significantly more likely than average to have wives ten or more years younger
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어

vocabulary.example: The new treatment significantly reduced recovery time.
College graduates are more likely than average to vote.

expressions.usage: 실증적 근거를 제시할 때 사용하는 학술/저널리즘 표현. data는 복수형이므로 show(shows 아님)를 취함

patterns.pattern: Data on [topic] show that [in year], [group] were significantly more likely than [benchmark] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 통계 데이터를 인용하여 특정 집단의 경향을 비교 제시하는 실증적 서술 패턴
examples: Data on hiring practices show that in 2010, women were significantly less likely than men to be promoted to senior roles.
Data on health outcomes show that in 2000, urban residents were significantly more likely than rural ones to access specialist care.

drillQuestions.question: Very rich men were significantly more ______ than average to have much younger wives.

sentences.notes: data는 라틴어 복수형이므로 격식적 글에서는 show를 사용(shows 아님). significantly more likely than average는 통계적 비교의 핵심 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: wives ten or more years younger

sentences.original: The stereotype of the older, powerful man with a glamorous young spouse was grounded in real numbers.
sentences.translation: 나이 많고 권력 있는 남성이 화려한 젊은 배우자를 둔다는 고정관념은 실제 수치에 근거하고 있었다.

grammarElements.text: The stereotype of the older, powerful man with a glamorous young spouse
grammarElements.label: 주어 (이중 전치사구 수식)

vocabulary.example: The media often reinforces harmful stereotypes about minority groups.
The glamorous lifestyle of movie stars fascinates many people.
His arguments were grounded in solid research.

expressions.usage: 추상적 인식이 실증적 데이터로 뒷받침됨을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The stereotype of [description] was grounded in [evidence]
patterns.explanation: 고정관념이 단순한 편견이 아니라 실제 근거가 있었음을 인정하는 객관적 서술 구조
examples: The stereotype of the workaholic Japanese salaryman was grounded in real working-hour data.
The stereotype of the American dream was grounded in actual upward mobility statistics.

drillQuestions.question: The stereotype was ______ in real numbers.

sentences.notes: grounded in은 '~에 근거한'이라는 뜻으로, 고정관념이 단순한 편견이 아니라 통계적 현실에 기반했음을 인정하는 균형 잡힌 서술. was grounded(수동)는 과거 상태를 나타냄.

koreanAlignment.en: The stereotype of the older, powerful man
koreanAlignment.ko: 나이 많고 권력 있는 남성이라는 고정관념

sentences.original: But recent decades have brought a dramatic shift.
sentences.translation: 그러나 최근 수십 년은 극적인 변화를 가져왔다.

vocabulary.example: The company has undergone a dramatic transformation in the past five years.
There has been a notable shift in public opinion.

expressions.usage: 시간의 흐름이 주어가 되어 큰 변화를 초래했음을 표현하는 패턴

patterns.pattern: But [time period] has/have brought a dramatic [noun]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 내용과 대조하며 최근의 큰 변화를 선언하는 짧은 전환 문장
examples: But the last decade has brought a dramatic reversal.
But recent years have brought a dramatic reassessment of that assumption.

drillQuestions.question: Recent decades have brought a dramatic ______.

sentences.notes: 짧고 강렬한 전환 문장. But으로 시작하여 앞선 '오래된 패턴'과의 단절을 선언한다. 현재완료(have brought)는 최근까지 이어진 변화를 나타냄.

sentences.original: By 2024, the wealthiest men no longer stood out from the general population in terms of spousal age gaps.
sentences.translation: 2024년까지 가장 부유한 남성들은 배우자 나이 차이 측면에서 더 이상 일반 인구와 구별되지 않았다.

grammarElements.text: in terms of spousal age gaps
grammarElements.label: 기준 전치사구

vocabulary.example: Her performance stood out from the rest of the team.
Spousal support is a common topic in divorce proceedings.

expressions.usage: 이전에는 차이가 있었으나 이제는 사라졌음을 나타내는 표현. no longer가 변화의 완료를 강조
특정 기준이나 척도를 명시할 때 사용하는 핵심 전치사구

patterns.pattern: By [year], [group] no longer [verb-ed] from [comparison group] in terms of [metric]
patterns.explanation: 특정 시점까지 기존 차이가 사라졌음을 데이터로 보여주는 시간적 완료 패턴
examples: By 2020, rural schools no longer lagged behind urban ones in terms of internet access.
By the end of the decade, developing nations no longer trailed behind in terms of mobile adoption.

drillQuestions.question: The wealthiest men no longer stood out from the general population in ______ of spousal age gaps.

sentences.notes: By 2024는 '2024년까지'로, 변화가 완료된 시점을 명시한다. in terms of는 비교의 기준을 제시하는 핵심 표현으로, 학술 및 저널리즘 문체에서 필수.

koreanAlignment.en: no longer stood out from the general population
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 이상 일반 인구와 구별되지 않았다

koreanAlignment.en: in terms of spousal age gaps

sentences.original: The old model had quietly faded.
sentences.translation: 낡은 모델은 조용히 사라졌다.

vocabulary.example: The once-popular trend had gradually faded from public consciousness.

expressions.usage: 큰 주목 없이 서서히 소멸한 현상을 묘사할 때 사용. quietly는 변화의 눈에 띄지 않는 성격을 강조

patterns.pattern: The old [noun] had quietly [past participle]
patterns.explanation: 과거완료를 사용하여 이전의 것이 이미 서서히 사라졌음을 나타내는 간결한 마무리 구조
examples: The old consensus had quietly dissolved.
The old rivalry had quietly disappeared.

drillQuestions.question: The old model had quietly ______.

sentences.notes: 문단의 마무리 문장으로, 의도적으로 짧게 작성하여 여운을 남긴다. 과거완료(had faded)는 '극적인 변화가 있기 전 이미 사라져 있었다'는 시간적 선후를 나타냄. quietly가 핵심으로, 조용하고 눈에 띄지 않는 변화를 암시.

sentences.original: Several forces drove this change.
sentences.translation: 여러 힘이 이 변화를 이끌었다.

vocabulary.example: Market forces eventually corrected the imbalance.
Innovation drove economic growth throughout the century.

expressions.usage: 변화의 원인이 되는 요인을 소개할 때 사용. drive는 cause보다 적극적 뉘앙스

patterns.pattern: Several forces drove [this change/transformation]
patterns.explanation: 변화의 원인을 분석하기 시작하는 문단 도입 구조. 이후 구체적 원인들이 열거됨
examples: Several forces drove this transformation in healthcare.
Several forces drove the rapid adoption of remote work.

drillQuestions.question: Several ______ drove this change.

sentences.notes: 새 문단의 주제 문장으로, 앞서 언급한 '변화'의 원인을 분석하겠다는 예고. 짧고 선언적인 구조가 독자의 주의를 환기시킨다. drive-drove-driven 불규칙 변화에 주의.

sentences.original: As more women pursued higher education and built independent careers, they gained the freedom to choose partners based on shared interests and compatible lifestyles rather than financial need.
sentences.translation: 더 많은 여성이 고등교육을 받고 독립적인 경력을 쌓으면서, 그들은 재정적 필요가 아닌 공유하는 관심사와 양립 가능한 생활방식에 기초하여 파트너를 선택할 자유를 얻었다.

grammarElements.text: As more women pursued higher education and built independent careers
grammarElements.label: as 시간/이유절 (병렬 동사)

grammarElements.text: the freedom to choose partners based on shared interests and compatible lifestyles rather than financial need
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (to부정사 + rather than 대조)

vocabulary.example: She pursued a degree in engineering despite family objections.
They discovered that their lifestyles were highly compatible.

expressions.usage: 선택의 기준이 바뀌었음을 대조적으로 제시하는 표현. rather than이 기존 기준을 부정
이전에는 불가능했던 것이 가능해진 변화를 표현

patterns.pattern: As [group] [verb-ed] and [verb-ed], they gained the freedom to [verb] based on [new criteria] rather than [old criteria]
patterns.explanation: 특정 집단의 사회적 변화가 선택 기준의 전환으로 이어졌음을 서술하는 인과 구조
examples: As workers gained mobility and built diverse skills, they gained the freedom to choose employers based on culture rather than salary alone.
As citizens gained access to information and built civic networks, they gained the freedom to demand accountability based on evidence rather than tradition.

drillQuestions.question: They gained the freedom to choose partners based on shared interests and ______ lifestyles rather than financial need.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 글의 핵심 논지를 담고 있다. rather than은 '~가 아니라'로, 기존 기준(돈)에서 새 기준(공유 관심사)으로의 전환을 명시적으로 대조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: As more women pursued higher education
gained the freedom to choose partners
based on shared interests and compatible lifestyles
koreanAlignment.ko: 공유하는 관심사와 양립 가능한 생활방식에 기초하여

sentences.original: People who spend years in university tend to form relationships with classmates close to their own age.
sentences.translation: 대학에서 수년을 보내는 사람들은 자기 나이에 가까운 동급생과 관계를 형성하는 경향이 있다.

grammarElements.text: People who spend years in university
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: relationships with classmates close to their own age
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (형용사구 후치 수식)

vocabulary.example: People tend to remember negative experiences more vividly.
Children form relationships easily through play.

expressions.usage: 나이 차이가 크지 않음을 표현. close to는 '~에 가까운'이라는 근접 표현

patterns.pattern: People who [activity] tend to [verb] [relationship] with [peers] close to [characteristic]
patterns.explanation: 특정 활동을 하는 사람들의 사회적 경향을 일반화하는 구조
examples: People who work in startups tend to form networks with colleagues close to their own level of experience.
People who travel frequently tend to build friendships with others close to their own interests.

drillQuestions.question: People who spend years in university ______ to form relationships with classmates close to their own age.

sentences.notes: tend to는 '~하는 경향이 있다'는 뜻으로 일반적 경향을 서술하는 핵심 표현. close to their own age에서 close는 형용사로 classmates를 후치 수식한다.

koreanAlignment.en: People who spend years in university
classmates close to their own age

sentences.original: Education and evolving social attitudes have replaced wealth as the primary factors shaping who pairs up with whom.
sentences.translation: 교육과 변화하는 사회적 태도가 누가 누구와 짝을 이루는지를 결정하는 주요 요인으로서 부를 대체했다.

grammarElements.text: as the primary factors shaping who pairs up with whom
grammarElements.label: as 보어 (현재분사 + 간접의문문)

vocabulary.example: The evolving landscape of technology keeps businesses on their toes.
The primary goal of the program is to reduce poverty.

expressions.usage: 기존의 것을 새로운 것이 대체했음을 표현하는 구조. replace A as B / replace A with B 모두 가능
연인 관계 형성을 가볍게 표현하는 구동사

patterns.pattern: [New factor] has replaced [old factor] as the primary [noun] shaping [outcome]
patterns.explanation: 기존 요인이 새로운 요인으로 대체되었음을 선언하는 구조. 글의 핵심 주장을 담는 패턴
examples: Skill has replaced seniority as the primary criterion shaping promotions.
Sustainability has replaced cost as the primary factor shaping consumer choices.

drillQuestions.question: Education and evolving social attitudes have ______ wealth as the primary factors.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 주장을 담은 문장. replace A as B 구문은 '~로서 A를 대체하다'라는 뜻. who pairs up with whom에서 whom은 전치사 with의 목적어로 격식적 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: shaping who pairs up with whom

sentences.original: Meanwhile, women who earn high salaries generally do not seek significantly younger husbands, suggesting that economic power alone does not automatically produce large age gaps in either direction.
sentences.translation: 한편, 높은 연봉을 받는 여성들은 일반적으로 현저히 젊은 남편을 찾지 않는데, 이는 경제력만으로는 어느 방향으로든 자동적으로 큰 나이 차이를 만들어내지 않음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: suggesting that economic power alone does not automatically produce large age gaps in either direction
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문 (that절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The results suggest that the treatment is effective.
Having money does not automatically guarantee happiness.

expressions.usage: 양쪽 모두에 해당하는 결론을 내릴 때 사용하는 표현. 남성-여성 어느 쪽이든이라는 양방향성을 강조
하나의 요인만으로는 충분하지 않다는 한계를 강조. alone이 명사 뒤에 위치

patterns.pattern: Meanwhile, [group] generally do not [verb], suggesting that [broad conclusion]
patterns.explanation: 반대 방향의 사례를 제시하여 더 넓은 결론을 도출하는 추론 패턴
examples: Meanwhile, wealthy nations generally do not outperform in happiness rankings, suggesting that GDP alone does not determine well-being.
Meanwhile, highly educated parents generally do not produce higher-scoring children, suggesting that schooling alone does not guarantee academic success.

drillQuestions.question: Economic power alone does not ______ produce large age gaps in either direction.

sentences.notes: suggesting은 분사구문으로 앞 문장 전체의 결과/함의를 제시한다. in either direction은 '남성이든 여성이든' 양방향을 의미하여 논리적 균형을 맞춘다.

koreanAlignment.en: generally do not seek significantly younger husbands
koreanAlignment.ko: 일반적으로 현저히 젊은 남편을 찾지 않는다

sentences.original: From Roman grooming manuals to Gilded Age marriage markets to modern data on couples, one thread runs through every era: people care deeply about attracting and keeping a partner.
sentences.translation: 로마 시대의 몸단장 안내서에서 도금 시대의 결혼 시장, 현대의 커플 관련 데이터에 이르기까지, 하나의 공통점이 모든 시대를 관통한다: 사람들은 파트너를 끌어들이고 유지하는 것에 깊이 관심을 가진다.

grammarElements.text: From Roman grooming manuals to Gilded Age marriage markets to modern data on couples
grammarElements.label: from A to B to C 병렬 부사구

grammarElements.text: people care deeply about attracting and keeping a partner
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 부연 설명절

vocabulary.example: A common thread connects all of her novels.
A sense of optimism runs through the entire book.

expressions.phrase: From A to B to C
expressions.usage: 시간적 또는 공간적 범위를 세 단계로 나열하여 포괄성을 강조하는 표현
단순한 관심을 넘어선 진지한 몰입을 표현

patterns.pattern: From [earliest example] to [middle example] to [latest example], one [noun] runs through every [time unit]: [core truth]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 범위를 세 단계로 제시한 뒤 공통점을 콜론으로 선언하는 결론부 도입 패턴
examples: From ancient scrolls to medieval manuscripts to digital archives, one principle runs through every medium: humans need to record their stories.
From village markets to industrial factories to online platforms, one impulse runs through every era: the desire to trade.

drillQuestions.question: One ______ runs through every era: people care deeply about attracting and keeping a partner.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 도입문으로, 글 전체의 역사적 범위를 from-to-to 구조로 요약한다. thread는 '실'이라는 원래 뜻에서 '관통하는 공통 요소'라는 비유적 의미로 확장. 콜론(:) 뒤에 핵심 메시지 제시.

koreanAlignment.en: one thread runs through every era
people care deeply about attracting and keeping a partner
koreanAlignment.ko: 사람들은 파트너를 끌어들이고 유지하는 데 깊이 관심을 가진다

sentences.original: The specific rules change with each generation, but the underlying human impulse remains constant.
sentences.translation: 구체적인 규칙은 세대마다 변하지만, 근본적인 인간의 충동은 변하지 않는다.

grammarElements.text: but the underlying human impulse remains constant
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: The underlying cause of the problem was never addressed.
The impulse to help others seems deeply rooted in human nature.
Despite all the changes, her dedication remained constant.

expressions.usage: 변화 속에서도 불변하는 것을 강조할 때 사용하는 연결동사 표현

patterns.pattern: The specific [noun] change with each [unit], but the underlying [noun] remains constant
patterns.explanation: 표면적 변화와 근본적 불변을 대조하는 균형 잡힌 but 구조. 결론부에서 자주 사용
examples: The specific technologies change with each decade, but the underlying need for connection remains constant.
The specific fashions change with each season, but the underlying desire for self-expression remains constant.

drillQuestions.question: The underlying human ______ remains constant.

sentences.notes: 변화(change)와 불변(remains constant)의 대조가 글의 이중 메시지를 한 문장으로 압축한다. underlying은 '표면 아래에 있는, 근본적인'이라는 뜻으로, surface-level과 대비.

sentences.original: What is different today is that partnership increasingly rests on equality and personal connection rather than on displays of wealth and status.
sentences.translation: 오늘날 다른 점은 파트너십이 부와 지위의 과시가 아니라 점점 더 평등과 개인적 유대에 기반하고 있다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: that partnership increasingly rests on equality and personal connection rather than on displays of wealth and status
grammarElements.label: that절 보어

vocabulary.example: Consumers are increasingly choosing sustainable products.
The success of the project rests on careful planning.

expressions.phrase: What is different today is that
expressions.usage: 글 전체의 핵심 결론을 의사분열문으로 강조하는 표현. 2문단의 What has changed와 대응
두 기반을 대조할 때 전치사 on을 반복하여 문법적 병렬을 유지하는 정교한 표현

patterns.pattern: What is different today is that [trend] increasingly rests on [new value] rather than on [old value]
patterns.explanation: 현재의 변화된 상황을 의사분열문으로 강조하고 rather than으로 과거와 대조하는 결론 패턴
examples: What is different today is that career success increasingly rests on adaptability rather than on seniority.
What is different today is that political legitimacy increasingly rests on transparency rather than on authority.

drillQuestions.question: Partnership increasingly ______ on equality and personal connection.

sentences.notes: 2문단의 'What has changed'와 수미상관을 이루는 의사분열문. rather than on에서 on의 반복은 rests on과의 문법적 병렬을 유지하기 위한 것으로, 세련된 문체의 표지.

koreanAlignment.en: rather than on displays of wealth and status

pronunciationNotes: increasingly [ɪnˈkriːsɪŋli] -- '인크리이싱리'로 두 번째 음절 강세와 장모음

sentences.original: That quiet revolution may be the most significant shift in the long history of romance.
sentences.translation: 그 조용한 혁명은 기나긴 연애의 역사에서 가장 중요한 변화일 것이다.

grammarElements.text: the most significant shift in the long history of romance
grammarElements.label: 보어 (최상급 + 전치사구)

vocabulary.example: The digital revolution transformed how people communicate.
This is the most significant discovery in the field in decades.

expressions.usage: 큰 소란 없이 서서히 일어났지만 근본적인 변화를 가리키는 표현. 역설적 조합(oxymoron)이 인상적

patterns.pattern: That [adjective] [noun] may be the most [superlative adjective] [noun] in the [long/entire] history of [field]
patterns.explanation: 글의 마지막 문장에서 전체 논의를 역사적 맥락 속에 자리매김하는 압축적 결론 구조
examples: That gradual awakening may be the most profound transformation in the long history of education.
That silent shift may be the most consequential change in the entire history of labor relations.

drillQuestions.question: That quiet ______ may be the most significant shift in the long history of romance.

sentences.notes: 글의 마지막 문장. quiet revolution은 역설적 조합(oxymoron)으로, 혁명은 보통 시끄럽지만 이 변화는 조용히 일어났음을 강조한다. may be는 단정이 아닌 조심스러운 평가를 나타내어, 학술적 절제를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: may be the most significant shift
in the long history of romance

pronunciationNotes: revolution [ˌrevəˈluːʃn] -- '레버루우션'으로 세 번째 음절 강세와 장모음
